# Employment Agreement

**Parties:** Sandbourne Industries Inc. ("Employer") and Jordan Fairweather ("Employee")

This Employment Agreement is entered into between Sandbourne Industries Inc., a corporation
incorporated under the laws of Alberta ("Employer"), and Jordan Fairweather, an individual
residing in Calgary, Alberta ("Employee"), effective as of April 1, 2026.

## Position and Duties

The Employer shall employ the Employee as Director of Operations. The Employee shall
report directly to the Chief Executive Officer of Sandbourne Industries Inc. and shall
perform such duties as are assigned by the Chief Executive Officer from time to time.

The Employee shall devote substantially all of the Employee's working time and attention
to the performance of duties under this Agreement and shall not, without the prior written
consent of the Employer, engage in any outside employment or business activities that
conflict with the Employee's obligations hereunder.

## Compensation and Benefits

The Employer shall pay the Employee a base salary of $185,000 per annum, payable in equal
semi-monthly instalments in accordance with the Employer's payroll practices.

The Employee shall be eligible to participate in the Employer's annual performance bonus
plan, with a target bonus of 20% of base salary, payable no later than March 31 of each
calendar year following the performance year, subject to the Employee remaining employed
as of the payment date.

The Employer shall provide the Employee with group benefits coverage, including extended
health, dental, and life insurance, in accordance with Sandbourne Industries Inc.'s standard
benefits plan as amended from time to time.

## Term and Termination

This Agreement shall commence on April 1, 2026, and shall continue until terminated in
accordance with this Article.

### Termination Without Cause

The Employer may terminate the Employee's employment without cause by providing the
Employee with working notice or pay in lieu of notice equal to:

- One month of base salary for each complete year of service, with a minimum notice
  period of 4 months and a maximum of 18 months.

The Employer shall provide the Employee with written notice of termination without cause
no later than the effective date of termination.

### Resignation by Employee

The Employee shall provide the Employer with no less than 30 days prior written notice
of the Employee's intention to resign. During the notice period, the Employee shall
continue to perform the Employee's duties and shall cooperate with the transition of
the Employee's responsibilities.

### Termination for Cause

The Employer may terminate this Agreement immediately for cause without notice or payment
in lieu of notice upon: (i) material breach of this Agreement by the Employee; (ii) willful
misconduct, fraud, or dishonesty in the performance of duties; or (iii) conviction of a
criminal offence.

If the Employer alleges cause based on performance-related conduct, the Employer shall
first provide the Employee with written notice specifying the alleged deficiency in detail.
The Employee shall have 15 business days after receipt of such written notice to cure the
deficiency to the reasonable satisfaction of the Employer. If the Employee fails to cure
within the 15-business-day cure period, the Employer may terminate for cause.

## Non-Solicitation

During the term of employment and for a period of 12 months following termination, the
Employee shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit or attempt to solicit any customer of
Sandbourne Industries Inc. with whom the Employee had material contact during the 24 months
preceding termination.

During the term of employment and for a period of 6 months following termination, the
Employee shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit, hire, or attempt to hire any employee
of Sandbourne Industries Inc.

## Confidentiality

The Employee agrees to keep confidential all Confidential Information of Sandbourne Industries
Inc. both during and after the term of employment. "Confidential Information" means all
non-public business, technical, financial, and strategic information of the Employer, including
trade secrets, customer lists, pricing information, and business plans.

The Employee must immediately notify the Employer in writing upon becoming aware of any
actual or suspected unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information.

The obligation of confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement indefinitely
with respect to trade secrets and for 5 years with respect to other Confidential Information.

## Deductions and Source Deductions

The Employer shall make all deductions required by law, including income tax, Canada Pension
Plan contributions, and Employment Insurance premiums, from amounts payable to the Employee
under this Agreement.

## Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province
of Alberta and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein.

Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of
the courts of the Province of Alberta.
